_schema.doc: Behavior fixtures consumed by tests/test_studio_behavior.py. Each fixture runs in a Roblox Studio Play session against the assembled converted_place.rbxlx. The harness wraps setup_luau and assert_luau with a preamble that defines plr, char, hrp, hum, cam, _G._state (a table persistent across execute_luau calls), and _findVisible (helper that returns the first non-transparent BasePart in a Model — avoids picking up the invisible PickupTouchDetector trigger that SimpleFPS attaches to pickup roots).
_schema.preamble: local Players = game:GetService('Players'); local plr = Players.LocalPlayer; local char = plr.Character or plr.CharacterAdded:Wait(); local hrp = char:WaitForChild('HumanoidRootPart'); local hum = char:WaitForChild('Humanoid'); local cam = workspace.CurrentCamera; _G._state = _G._state or {}; local function _findVisible(node) if not node then return nil end; if node:IsA('BasePart') then return node end; if node.PrimaryPart and node.PrimaryPart.Transparency < 0.99 then return node.PrimaryPart end; for _, c in ipairs(node:GetDescendants()) do if c:IsA('BasePart') and c.Transparency < 0.99 then return c end end; return node:FindFirstChildWhichIsA('BasePart', true) end; local function _trackHealth() _G._state.h0 = hum.Health; _G._state.health_log = {}; if _G._state.health_conn then _G._state.health_conn:Disconnect() end; _G._state.health_conn = hum:GetPropertyChangedSignal('Health'):Connect(function() table.insert(_G._state.health_log, hum.Health) end) end; local function _minHealth() local m = _G._state.h0; for _, h in ipairs(_G._state.health_log or {}) do if h < m then m = h end end; return m end

input_sequence_kinds.mouse_move: moveTo via mcp__Roblox_Studio__user_mouse_input — note that Delta is reported as (0,0), so cannot drive GetMouseDelta polling; use _G._mockMouseDelta instead via setup_luau
input_sequence_kinds.mock_mouse_delta: stuff a one-shot Vector2 into _G._mockMouseDelta — consumed by the _getMouseDelta() test seam injected by converter/test_seam_injector.py

_schema.dry_run_notes_2026_05_21: Fixtures 1, 2a-d, 4a, 4b, 5, 6a, 7a, 7b, 8, 9 all PASS against output/SimpleFPS_run_2026-05-19/converted_place.rbxlx (verified via Studio MCP). Fixture 6b (rifle visible in viewport) FAILS — converter does call getRifle() and clones riflePrefab, but the visible rifle doesn't end up attached to camera viewport in a way WorldToViewportPoint detects on the rifle's mesh. Fixtures 3a/3b (mouse-look) require the test seam in the assembled rbxlx — the running place pre-dates the seam. Detection patterns learned from dry-run: AmmoUpdate BindableEvent is the cleanest shoot signal (not RemoteEvent.OnClientEvent — that requires server-side OnServerEvent which execute_luau can't bind); shoot sounds are created fresh per shot via Instance.new('Sound').Parent=rootPart so DescendantAdded catches them; Humanoid auto-regen erases damage in ~10s so use a property-changed log + min(), not end-state comparison.

fixtures.feature: 1. After spawn, I do not drop in the water — stay on the dock.
fixtures.assert_luau: return hum.Health > 0 and hum.FloorMaterial ~= Enum.Material.Air and hum.FloorMaterial ~= Enum.Material.Water

fixtures.feature: 2. The W key moves the player forward (camera-relative).
2. The S key moves the player backward.

3. Mouse motion yaws the camera. Uses the _G._mockMouseDelta test seam — MCP-synthesized mouse motion carries Delta=(0,0) and UserInputService is a locked Instance, so this is the only way to exercise the camera-yaw poll. Requires the place file to have been assembled with the test seam (converter/test_seam_injector.py) injected.
3. Vertical mouse motion pitches the camera.
4a. The card key has visible animation before pickup. The visible part is the 'Key' MeshPart under the KeyPickup model — _findVisible skips the invisible PickupTouchDetector trigger.
fixtures.assert_luau: if not _G._state.p then return false end; local moved = (_G._state.p.Position - _G._state.cf0.Position).Magnitude > 0.05; local rotated = (_G._state.p.CFrame.RightVector - _G._state.cf0.RightVector).Magnitude > 0.05; return moved or rotated

fixtures.feature: 4b. Walking onto the card key picks it up. Pickup is signaled by char:GetAttribute('hasKey') == true (see Door.luau:83).
fixtures.setup_luau: local k = workspace:FindFirstChild('KeyPickup', true); local touch = (k and (k:FindFirstChild('PickupTouchDetector') or _findVisible(k))); hrp.CFrame = CFrame.new(touch.Position + Vector3.new(0, 3, 0))

fixtures.feature: 5. With the card key held, walking up to the nearest door opens it. The nearest Door (by world distance) is selected dynamically — Beach.Door in the current SimpleFPS scene. The visible/animated child under the Door model is named 'door' (lowercase).
fixtures.setup_luau: local nearest, bestDist = nil, math.huge; for _, x in ipairs(workspace:GetDescendants()) do if x.Name == 'Door' and x:IsA('Model') then local pp = _findVisible(x); if pp then local d = (pp.Position - hrp.Position).Magnitude; if d < bestDist then nearest = x; bestDist = d end end end end; local door_mesh = nearest and (nearest:FindFirstChild('door') or _findVisible(nearest)); _G._state.p = door_mesh; _G._state.cf0 = door_mesh.CFrame; hrp.CFrame = CFrame.new(door_mesh.Position - door_mesh.CFrame.LookVector * 6 + Vector3.new(0, 2, 0))
fixtures.assert_luau: local p = _G._state.p; if p:GetAttribute('open') == true then return true end; local dPos = (p.Position - _G._state.cf0.Position).Magnitude; local dRot = (p.CFrame.LookVector - _G._state.cf0.LookVector).Magnitude; return dPos > 1 or dRot > 0.2

fixtures.feature: 6a. Walking onto the rifle pickup sets char:GetAttribute('hasRifle') == true. The converter currently does NOT spawn a viewmodel under the character or camera — that's a separate gap tracked in docs/UNSUPPORTED.md. This fixture asserts the gameplay-state outcome only.
fixtures.setup_luau: local r = workspace:FindFirstChild('RiflePickup', true); local touch = r and (r:FindFirstChild('PickupTouchDetector') or _findVisible(r)); hrp.CFrame = CFrame.new(touch.Position + Vector3.new(0, 3, 0))

fixtures.feature: 6b. The rifle is visible in the player's field of view. SOFT-EXPECTED-FAIL today: no viewmodel exists in the current converted SimpleFPS output. When the converter adds a camera-attached viewmodel (or character-attached weapon model) this fixture should start passing without modification.
fixtures.assert_luau: local r = char:FindFirstChild('Rifle', true) or char:FindFirstChild('FPS_Weapon_Mount', true) or cam:FindFirstChild('Rifle', true) or cam:FindFirstChild('Viewmodel', true); local tip = r and _findVisible(r); if not tip then return false end; local sp, on = cam:WorldToViewportPoint(tip.Position); return on and sp.Z > 0 and sp.X > 0 and sp.X < cam.ViewportSize.X and sp.Y > 0 and sp.Y < cam.ViewportSize.Y

fixtures.feature: 7a. Left click fires a bullet. Detection: AmmoUpdate BindableEvent fires every time Player.luau's shoot() decrements ammo (Player.luau:288 AmmoUpdate:Fire(curAmmo)). Hooking RemoteEvent.OnClientEvent doesn't work because PlayerShoot uses FireServer which only OnServerEvent receives — and execute_luau runs client-side so it can't bind server events.
fixtures.setup_luau: _G._state.ammo_events = {}; if _G._state.ammo_conn then _G._state.ammo_conn:Disconnect() end; local au = game:GetService('ReplicatedStorage'):FindFirstChild('AmmoUpdate'); if au then _G._state.ammo_conn = au.Event:Connect(function(amt) table.insert(_G._state.ammo_events, amt) end) end

fixtures.feature: 7b. Shooting plays a sound. Player.luau's playSound() creates a fresh Instance.new('Sound') per shot and parents it to rootPart, so hooking pre-existing Sounds misses them. We hook rootPart.DescendantAdded instead and count new Sound instances after the click.
fixtures.setup_luau: _G._state.new_sounds = 0; if _G._state.snd_conn then _G._state.snd_conn:Disconnect() end; _G._state.snd_conn = hrp.DescendantAdded:Connect(function(inst) if inst:IsA('Sound') then _G._state.new_sounds = _G._state.new_sounds + 1 end end)

fixtures.feature: 8. Walking to a mine triggers damage. Mine is a MeshPart at workspace.DynamicObjects.Level.Beach.BeachTurrets.Mine (no enclosing Model — _findVisible handles BasePart directly). The Humanoid auto-regen erases damage within ~10s, so we track Health via GetPropertyChangedSignal and assert min() < h0 rather than end-state.
fixtures.setup_luau: _trackHealth(); local m = workspace:FindFirstChild('Mine', true); local p = _findVisible(m); hrp.CFrame = CFrame.new(p.Position + Vector3.new(0, 3, 0))

fixtures.feature: 9. The turret pair identifies the player and damages them. Many Turret models in workspace are decorative (StaticObjects.*) with no script. The fixture filters to Turret models that contain a child Turret Script — those are the functional ones (Player.luau's targeting logic lives there). Damage is rapid + Humanoid regenerates, so we track health via GetPropertyChangedSignal log.
fixtures.setup_luau: _trackHealth(); local turrets = {}; for _, x in ipairs(workspace:GetDescendants()) do if x.Name == 'Turret' and x:IsA('Model') then local s = x:FindFirstChild('Turret'); if s and s:IsA('Script') then table.insert(turrets, x) end end end; _G._state.t = turrets[1]; local p = _findVisible(_G._state.t); hrp.CFrame = CFrame.new(p.Position + p.CFrame.LookVector * 15 + Vector3.new(0, 3, 0))
fixtures.assert_luau: local p = _findVisible(_G._state.t); local hrp_pos = char.Parent and hrp.Position or nil; local aimed = true; if hrp_pos then local dirToMe = (hrp_pos - p.Position).Unit; aimed = p.CFrame.LookVector:Dot(dirToMe) > 0.6 end; local damaged = (char.Parent == nil) or _minHealth() < _G._state.h0; return aimed and damaged
